content:
**Boston Celtics Clinch 2025 NBA Eastern Conference Championship, Advance to Finals**

*June 5, 2025 | Boston, MA* — The Boston Celtics have secured the 2025 NBA Eastern Conference Championship, defeating the Milwaukee Bucks 4-2 in a thrilling series that showcased the team’s depth and resilience. With last night’s 112-106 victory at TD Garden, Boston punched its ticket to the NBA Finals for the second time in four years.

Jayson Tatum, who averaged 28.4 points in the series, was named Eastern Conference Finals MVP. “We’ve been so close these past few years, and to finally get over the hump feels amazing. We owe it to our fans and this city,” Tatum said after the game, holding the Bob Cousy Trophy.

Boston’s defense was key, as Jrue Holiday and Derrick White contained Milwaukee’s superstar Giannis Antetokounmpo, holding him to just 23 points in the decisive Game 6. “We knew it wouldn’t be easy. Milwaukee is a great team, but our guys stayed locked in,” Celtics head coach Joe Mazzulla commented.

The Celtics now look ahead to a Finals matchup against the defending champion Denver Nuggets. Fans flooded Causeway Street in celebration, hopeful that this year’s squad can deliver Boston’s 18th NBA title. “This team is special,” said longtime fan Linda Morales, “and this city believes.”
